title: rubus serpens var. multisetus
intro: Rubus serpens var. multisetus is a rare and lesser-known variant of the Rubus serpens species, commonly known as the creeping thimbleberry. This perennial plant is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rubus serpens var. multisetus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce swelling and pain, and act as an antimicrobial agent. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
